controls.name: Out-of-band voice verification for payment approvals

scenarios.name: Internet-facing VPN zero-day threatening payroll operations
scenarios.technical_signal: Multiple internet scans identified a vulnerable VPN gateway exposed to the public internet. The software version matches a high-severity remote code execution CVE with active exploitation reported by industry sources.
scenarios.executive_trigger: A remotely exploitable edge vulnerability on the corporate VPN gateway
recommended_actions: Patch or isolate the VPN gateway within 24 hours.
Force credential reset for privileged remote access accounts.
Increase executive monitoring cadence until exposure is removed.

scenarios.name: Privileged identity compromise with lateral movement into customer support
scenarios.technical_signal: EDR and identity telemetry show suspicious PowerShell activity on an administrator workstation followed by impossible travel and unusual token reuse against customer support systems.
scenarios.executive_trigger: A likely compromise of a privileged administrator identity
recommended_actions: Disable the compromised accounts and revoke active sessions immediately.
Contain lateral movement paths into support infrastructure.
Brief senior leadership on potential customer-impacting delays.

scenarios.name: Cloud misconfiguration exposing regulated customer records
scenarios.technical_signal: A routine cloud posture scan identified a public storage bucket tied to an analytics workflow. The bucket contains customer onboarding exports and has permissive anonymous read access.
scenarios.executive_trigger: A cloud storage misconfiguration exposing regulated customer data
recommended_actions: Remove public access and rotate any exposed credentials.
Launch legal and privacy impact assessment within 24 hours.
Prepare an executive update on customer notification obligations.

scenarios.name: Supplier connectivity compromise disrupting fulfillment operations
scenarios.technical_signal: Threat monitoring and partner-side notifications indicate ransomware activity against a supplier account connected through the B2B VPN hub, with unusual file synchronization behavior observed in the warehouse scheduler environment.
scenarios.executive_trigger: A third-party compromise moving into the supplier connectivity path
recommended_actions: Suspend partner synchronization channels until integrity is confirmed.
Move warehouse operations to contingency workflows for the next business cycle.
Issue an executive operations update tied to order fulfillment risk.

scenarios.name: Deepfake executive impersonation targeting urgent payment approval
scenarios.technical_signal: Finance staff received an urgent voice note and follow-up collaboration message that convincingly impersonated the CFO and requested a confidential vendor payment reroute before quarter close. Message metadata, timing anomalies, and callback mismatch indicators suggest AI-generated voice spoofing and social engineering.
scenarios.executive_trigger: A likely deepfake impersonation of the CFO aimed at manipulating treasury approvals
recommended_actions: Freeze the requested payment workflow until executive identity is revalidated through a second channel.
Notify finance leadership and treasury operations of the impersonation attempt immediately.
Accelerate rollout of mandatory out-of-band approval checks for high-value payment changes.

scenarios.name: Weak administrator authentication exposing remote access path
scenarios.technical_signal: A control review identified privileged administrator accounts without enforced MFA on the remote access authentication service, increasing the chance of credential compromise against a business-critical entry point.
scenarios.executive_trigger: A privileged authentication control gap on the remote access platform
recommended_actions: Enforce MFA for all privileged remote access accounts immediately.
Review existing administrator sessions and reset exposed credentials.
Validate high-risk remote access paths until the control gap is closed.

scenarios.name: SQL Server remote code execution threatening finance data operations
scenarios.technical_signal: A critical SQL Server vulnerability with remote code execution characteristics is present on a finance database server that supports reporting and transaction integrity workflows.
scenarios.executive_trigger: A critical database vulnerability affecting the finance data platform
recommended_actions: Patch or isolate the affected SQL Server instance on an emergency timeline.
Restrict privileged administrative paths to the finance database tier.
Assess exposure of finance reporting and transaction integrity processes.

scenarios.name: Outdated internet-facing web server increasing customer portal attack surface
scenarios.technical_signal: A customer-facing web server is running an outdated software version with known vulnerabilities, expanding the attack surface for the public portal.
scenarios.executive_trigger: An outdated internet-facing web server supporting the customer portal
recommended_actions: Upgrade the affected web server to a supported version on the next urgent maintenance window.
Review WAF coverage and internet-exposed application routes.
Increase monitoring for web exploitation attempts until the version gap is removed.

scenarios.name: Ransomware outbreak threatening endpoint and customer operations
scenarios.technical_signal: Ransomware activity detected on endpoint workstations with evidence of file encryption and lateral spread toward customer-facing systems.
scenarios.executive_trigger: Active ransomware encryption detected on business endpoints
recommended_actions: Isolate affected endpoints from the network immediately to contain lateral spread.
Activate incident response plan and engage ransomware response retainer.
Assess backup integrity and prepare for potential restore operations.
Notify executive leadership of potential operational disruption timeline.

scenarios.name: Cloud hypervisor or container escape threatening workload integrity
scenarios.technical_signal: A critical vulnerability in the virtualisation or container platform allows an attacker to escape isolation boundaries and access host-level resources or adjacent workloads.
scenarios.executive_trigger: A hypervisor or container escape vulnerability exposing cloud workloads
recommended_actions: Apply vendor patch or mitigation for the hypervisor or container runtime vulnerability immediately.
Audit workloads sharing affected host infrastructure for signs of compromise.
Review cloud workload isolation boundaries and enforce least-privilege workload identities.
Escalate to cloud security and engineering leadership for containment scope assessment.

scenarios.name: Identity provider compromise enabling broad authentication bypass
scenarios.technical_signal: Indicators suggest the organisation's identity provider or SSO platform has been compromised, potentially allowing an attacker to forge authentication tokens and access any federated application.
scenarios.executive_trigger: A compromise of the centralised identity provider affecting all federated applications
recommended_actions: Revoke all active SSO sessions and force reauthentication across federated applications.
Rotate identity provider signing keys and audit recent authentication logs for anomalous access.
Notify application owners of all federated services and begin compromise scope review.
Engage identity provider vendor support and security team immediately.

scenarios.name: Email platform compromise enabling data access and impersonation
scenarios.technical_signal: A vulnerability or compromise of the email platform allows attacker access to mailboxes, calendar data, and internal communications, with potential for impersonation of senior staff.
scenarios.executive_trigger: A compromise of the corporate email platform affecting internal communications
recommended_actions: Apply emergency patch or disable exploited email platform functionality.
Audit recently accessed mailboxes for signs of exfiltration or forwarding rule tampering.
Reset credentials for all accounts with active email access and enforce MFA.
Issue executive advisory on the risk of email impersonation in pending communications.

scenarios.name: API credential exposure enabling unauthorised data access
scenarios.technical_signal: API keys, bearer tokens, or service credentials are exposed in source repositories or application responses, granting unauthorised actors access to backend services or customer data.
scenarios.executive_trigger: Exposed API credentials enabling unauthorised access to customer-facing services
recommended_actions: Rotate all exposed API keys, bearer tokens, and service credentials immediately.
Audit API access logs for signs of prior exploitation using the compromised credentials.
Implement secrets scanning in CI/CD pipelines to prevent future credential leakage.
Review API authentication controls and enforce token expiry and scope restrictions.

scenarios.name: Core network device compromise threatening connectivity and segmentation
scenarios.technical_signal: A critical vulnerability in a core network device such as a router or switch allows remote code execution or full device compromise, threatening network segmentation and connectivity.
scenarios.executive_trigger: A critical vulnerability on a core network device threatening infrastructure integrity
recommended_actions: Apply vendor emergency patch or isolate the affected network device from production traffic.
Audit network device configurations and access control lists for signs of tampering.
Validate network segmentation boundaries and review adjacent device integrity.
Notify network operations and security leadership of connectivity risk scope.

scenarios.name: OT/SCADA attack threatening operational and physical process continuity
scenarios.technical_signal: An attack targeting industrial control systems, SCADA platforms, or OT network components threatens physical process integrity and operational continuity.
scenarios.executive_trigger: A cyber attack on operational technology systems threatening physical process continuity
recommended_actions: Isolate affected OT network segments and initiate manual fallback procedures where possible.
Notify plant operations leadership and safety teams of potential process disruption.
Engage OT security specialists for forensic assessment of ICS/SCADA platform integrity.
Issue leadership update on production continuity risk and estimated recovery timeline.

scenarios.name: Unauthenticated database exposure enabling data exfiltration
scenarios.technical_signal: A database instance is exposed to the internet without authentication, allowing any actor to query or exfiltrate the full dataset.
scenarios.executive_trigger: An unauthenticated database exposed to the internet
recommended_actions: Block public access to the database immediately and enforce authentication.
Audit access logs for signs of unauthorised queries or data extraction.
Launch a data breach assessment to determine whether sensitive data was accessed.
Notify legal and privacy teams for regulatory impact assessment.

scenarios.name: SaaS account takeover via credential attack enabling data access
scenarios.technical_signal: Credential stuffing, password spraying, or phishing attacks are resulting in account takeover against cloud or SaaS-hosted business applications.
scenarios.executive_trigger: Account takeover activity targeting cloud business applications
recommended_actions: Force password resets and MFA enrollment for accounts showing compromise indicators.
Block suspicious IP ranges and implement adaptive authentication for affected applications.
Audit recently accessed data within compromised accounts for exfiltration risk.
Notify affected customers or employees if personal data may have been accessed.

scenarios.name: CI/CD pipeline compromise introducing malicious code into production
scenarios.technical_signal: A compromise of the software build pipeline, dependency manager, or CI/CD platform allows an attacker to inject malicious code into production deployments.
scenarios.executive_trigger: A compromised software build pipeline with potential for malicious code in production
recommended_actions: Halt production deployments from affected pipelines pending integrity review.
Audit recent pipeline runs and produced artefacts for signs of tampering.
Rotate pipeline credentials and review third-party dependency integrity.
Notify engineering leadership of potential production code integrity risk.
